seoTitle: Fennell Bay vs Fern Gully — Climate Risk Comparison | ClimateNest
seoDescription: Compare climate risk scores between Fennell Bay and Fern Gully. See which suburb is safer from flood, bushfire, heat, and storm hazards.
ogTitle: Fennell Bay vs Fern Gully: Climate Safety
ogDescription: Data-driven comparison of climate risk in Fennell Bay and Fern Gully.
heading: Fennell Bay vs Fern Gully — Which is Safer?
intro: Compare climate risk between Fennell Bay, New South Wales and Fern Gully, New South Wales. Our analysis uses data from BOM, CSIRO, and state hazard authorities to score each suburb across 6 climate hazards.
verdict: Fern Gully has a lower overall climate risk score (37/100) compared to Fennell Bay (39/100). We recommend getting an address-level report for properties in both suburbs before making a purchase decision.

faq.question: Is Fennell Bay or Fern Gully safer from flooding?
faq.answer: Fennell Bay has a flood risk score of 35/100, while Fern Gully scores 46/100. A lower score indicates lower flood risk. Get an address-level flood risk report for a specific property.

faq.question: Which suburb has lower bushfire risk?
faq.answer: Fennell Bay scores 33/100 for bushfire risk. Fern Gully scores 36/100. Fire risk depends on proximity to vegetation and local fire history.

faq.question: Should I buy in Fennell Bay or Fern Gully?
faq.answer: Both suburbs have climate considerations. Fern Gully has a lower overall climate risk score. We recommend getting a full ClimateNest report for any specific property you're considering, which includes 8 hazard scores, insurance projections, and actionable recommendations.

valueImpact.description: Properties in this area may face a 1.3% climate value drag over time due to rising insurance costs and climate adaptation expenses.
